annotations.label: Event

anchor.snippet: A point in spacetime is called an event

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of spacetime or its points (events).

annotations.label: World line

anchor.snippet: That path is called the particle's world line

annotations.note: Although Mathlib has `Path`, a particle's world line in spacetime is not formalized.

annotations.label: Spacetime as a manifold

anchor.snippet: Mathematically, spacetime is a manifold

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes the general manifold concept via `IsManifold`, but not spacetime specifically.

annotations.label: Observer in special relativity

anchor.snippet: an observer will, in most cases, mean a frame of reference

annotations.note: Frames of reference / observers in relativity are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Euclidean distance via Pythagorean theorem

anchor.snippet: between two points can be defined using the Pythagorean theorem

annotations.note: `EuclideanSpace.dist_eq` gives the distance as the square root of the sum of squared coordinate differences (Pythagorean form).

annotations.label: Invariance of spatial distance

anchor.snippet: the distance between the points will be the same for both

annotations.note: `Isometry.dist_eq` states isometries (e.g. rotations/translations) preserve distance, generalizing spatial-distance invariance.

annotations.label: Spacetime interval

anchor.snippet: Special relativity provides a new invariant, called the spacetime interval

annotations.note: The Minkowski spacetime interval quadratic form is not defined in Mathlib (its Minkowski refs are the gauge functional, not spacetime).

annotations.label: Invariance of the spacetime interval

anchor.snippet: The invariance of the spacetime interval between the same events for all inertial frames of reference is one of the fundamental results

annotations.note: Invariance of the Minkowski interval under Lorentz transformations is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Timelike, spacelike, lightlike intervals

anchor.snippet: the spacetime interval is referred to as timelike

annotations.note: The timelike/spacelike/lightlike classification of intervals is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tilt angle of the ct′ axis

anchor.snippet: the ct′ axis is tilted with respect to the ct axis by an angle θ given by

annotations.note: Minkowski-diagram axis tilt formulas are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Light cone

anchor.snippet: These two lines form what is called the light cone of the event O

annotations.note: Mathlib has convex cones but not the null/light cone of a spacetime metric.

annotations.label: Light cone divides spacetime

anchor.snippet: A light (double) cone divides spacetime into separate regions with respect to its apex

annotations.note: The partition of spacetime by the light cone is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Light cone and causality

anchor.snippet: The light cone has an essential role within the concept of causality

annotations.note: Causal structure of spacetime is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Invariant before–after ordering of timelike events

anchor.snippet: All observers will agree that for any given event, an event within the given event's future light cone occurs after the given event

annotations.note: Invariant causal ordering of timelike-separated events is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Relativity of simultaneity

anchor.snippet: is termed the relativity of simultaneity

annotations.note: Relativity of simultaneity is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Proper distance and proper time

anchor.snippet: A spacelike spacetime interval hence provides a measure of proper distance

annotations.note: Proper distance and proper time are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Invariant hyperbolae

anchor.snippet: which are termed invariant hyperbolae

annotations.note: Minkowski invariant hyperbolae are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Timelike vs spacelike hyperbolae

anchor.snippet: The magenta hyperbolae, which cross the x axis, are timelike curves

annotations.note: Classification of invariant hyperbolae as timelike/spacelike is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Time dilation

anchor.snippet: This illustrates the phenomenon known as time dilation

annotations.note: Time dilation is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Length contraction

anchor.snippet: Length contraction , like time dilation, is a manifestation of the relativity of simultaneity

annotations.note: Length contraction is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Mutual time dilation

anchor.snippet: Mutual time dilation and length contraction tend to strike beginners as inherently self-contradictory concepts

annotations.note: Mutual time dilation is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Twin paradox

anchor.snippet: The twin paradox is a thought experiment involving identical twins

annotations.note: The twin paradox is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Galilean transformation

anchor.snippet: the coordinate transformation is as follows

annotations.note: Galilean coordinate transformations are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Galilean addition of velocities

anchor.snippet: which is the common-sense Galilean law for the addition of velocities

annotations.note: The Galilean velocity-addition law is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Train and bullet thought experiment

anchor.snippet: Consider the following thought experiment: The red arrow illustrates a train

annotations.note: This physics thought experiment is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Einstein's velocity addition formula

anchor.snippet: to yield Einstein's formula for the addition of velocities

annotations.note: The relativistic velocity-addition formula is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Invariance of light speed under velocity addition

anchor.snippet: If u ′ is set equal to c , then the formula yields

annotations.note: Invariance of c under relativistic velocity addition is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lorentz factor

anchor.section: Time dilation and length contraction revisited
anchor.snippet: is called the Lorentz factor, denoted by the Greek letter gamma

annotations.note: The Lorentz factor γ is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: c is the maximum possible speed

anchor.section: Time dilation and length contraction revisited
anchor.snippet: implying that c is the maximum possible speed in nature

annotations.note: The speed-limit principle is a physics result not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lorentz transformations

anchor.snippet: Lorentz transformations are used to transform the coordinates of an event from one frame to another in special relativity

annotations.note: Lorentz transformations are not defined in Mathlib (its `Lorentz` refs concern Minkowski's gauge/geometry-of-numbers, unrelated).

annotations.label: Inverse Lorentz transformations

anchor.snippet: The inverse Lorentz transformations are:

annotations.note: Inverse Lorentz transformations are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lorentz reduces to Galilean at low speed

anchor.snippet: the Lorentz transformations approximate to the Galilean transformations

annotations.note: The low-speed limit of the Lorentz transformation is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Earth-to-Mars race

anchor.snippet: Example: Terence and Stella are at an Earth-to-Mars space race

annotations.note: This worked relativity example is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Principle of locality

anchor.snippet: the underlying principle of locality , which states that the influence that one particle exerts on another can not be transmitted instantaneously

annotations.note: The physical principle of locality is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: No transverse length contraction

anchor.snippet: There can be no such thing as length expansion/contraction in the transverse directions

annotations.note: The absence of transverse length contraction is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Linearity of Lorentz transformations

anchor.snippet: The Lorentz transformations have a mathematical property called linearity

annotations.note: Mathlib has general `LinearMap`, but the linearity of Lorentz transformations specifically is not formalized.

annotations.label: Composition of Lorentz transformations

anchor.snippet: if two Lorentz transformations are applied sequentially, the result is also a Lorentz transformation

annotations.note: Closure of Lorentz transformations under composition is not formalized (no Lorentz group in Mathlib).

annotations.label: Terence, Stella, Ursula composition

anchor.snippet: Example: Terence observes Stella speeding away from him at 0.500 c

annotations.note: This worked velocity-composition example is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Doppler effect

anchor.snippet: The Doppler effect is the change in frequency or wavelength of a wave for a receiver and source in relative motion

annotations.note: The Doppler effect is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Relativistic longitudinal Doppler effect

anchor.snippet: the relativistic Doppler effect is given by

annotations.note: The relativistic longitudinal Doppler formula is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Transverse Doppler effect

anchor.snippet: transverse Doppler shift is a relativistic effect that has no classical analog

annotations.note: The transverse Doppler effect is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Linear momentum

anchor.snippet: Linear momentum , the product of a particle's mass and velocity, is a vector quantity

annotations.note: Physical linear momentum is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Four-momentum extension

anchor.snippet: the momentum vector is extended to four dimensions

annotations.note: The four-dimensional extension of momentum is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Energy–momentum ratio of light

anchor.snippet: light carries energy and momentum, and that their ratio is a constant

annotations.note: The energy–momentum relation for light is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Photons are massless

anchor.snippet: meaning that photons are massless particles

annotations.note: The masslessness of photons is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Relativistic mass

anchor.snippet: the relativistic mass of an object increases with velocity according to the formula

annotations.note: Relativistic mass is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Invariant (rest) mass

anchor.snippet: is equal to the invariant length of the relativistic momentum vector

annotations.note: Invariant rest mass as the norm of four-momentum is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Four-momentum

anchor.snippet: the four-momentum (also called 4-momentum) is also called the energy–momentum 4-vector

annotations.note: The energy–momentum four-vector is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Conservation laws

anchor.snippet: conservation laws state that certain particular measurable properties of an isolated physical system do not change

annotations.note: Physical conservation laws are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Noether's theorem

anchor.snippet: Emmy Noether discovered that underlying each conservation law is a fundamental symmetry of nature

annotations.note: The symmetry–conservation Noether theorem is absent; Mathlib's `Noether` refs concern Noetherian rings/spaces.

annotations.label: Elastic and inelastic collisions

anchor.snippet: The two bodies rebound from each other in a completely elastic collision

annotations.note: Elastic/inelastic collisions are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fused mass exceeds sum of masses

anchor.snippet: is not equal to the sum of the invariant masses of the individual particles that collided

annotations.note: Non-additivity of invariant mass in collisions is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Center-of-momentum frame

anchor.snippet: This is the frame in which the space component of the system's total momentum is zero

annotations.note: The center-of-momentum frame is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Relativistic energy–momentum conservation

anchor.snippet: The relativistic conservation law for energy and momentum replaces the three classical conservation laws

annotations.note: Relativistic energy–momentum conservation is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Geodesic motion

anchor.snippet: We say that the satellite always follows along the path of a geodesic

annotations.note: No geodesic definition was found in Mathlib's Riemannian geometry files.

annotations.label: Coordinate independence

anchor.snippet: The first crucial concept is coordinate independence

annotations.note: Coordinate independence as a physics principle is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Equivalence principle

anchor.snippet: The equivalence principle states that in any sufficiently small region of space, the effects of gravitation are the same as those from acceleration

annotations.note: The equivalence principle is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Flat spacetime paradigm

anchor.snippet: The flat spacetime paradigm posits that matter creates a gravitational field that causes rulers to shrink

annotations.note: The flat-spacetime interpretation of gravity is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Poincaré group

anchor.snippet: The spacetime symmetry group for Special Relativity is the Poincaré group

annotations.note: The Poincaré group (isometry group of Minkowski space) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: BMS asymptotic symmetry group

anchor.snippet: their discovery of a rich infinite-dimensional group (the so-called BMS group) as the asymptotic symmetry group

annotations.note: The BMS asymptotic symmetry group is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Supertranslations

anchor.snippet: an additional infinity of transformation generators known as supertranslations

annotations.note: Supertranslation generators are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Riemannian geometry

anchor.snippet: Riemannian geometry is the branch of differential geometry that studies Riemannian manifolds

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes Riemannian manifolds/metrics, but 'Riemannian geometry' as a field is not a single statement.

annotations.label: Riemannian manifold (formal)

anchor.snippet: Riemannian geometry is the study of smooth manifolds with a Riemannian metric

annotations.note: `IsRiemannianManifold` captures a smooth manifold equipped with a Riemannian metric.

annotations.label: Spacetime continuum as Lorentzian manifold

anchor.snippet: a spacetime continuum is mathematically defined as a four-dimensional, smooth, connected Lorentzian manifold

annotations.note: Mathlib has Riemannian but not Lorentzian (indefinite-signature) manifolds.

annotations.label: Timelike, null, spacelike geodesics

anchor.snippet: Geodesics are said to be timelike, null, or spacelike if the tangent vector to one point of the geodesic is of this nature

annotations.note: Causal classification of geodesics is not formalized (no Lorentzian metric in Mathlib).

annotations.label: Spatial and temporal dimensions

anchor.snippet: There are two kinds of dimensions: spatial (bidirectional) and temporal (unidirectional)

annotations.note: The distinction between spatial and temporal dimensions is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ehrenfest orbital stability result

anchor.snippet: Paul Ehrenfest showed that if there is only a single time dimension and more than three spatial dimensions, the orbit of a planet about its Sun cannot remain stable

annotations.note: Ehrenfest's orbital-stability dimensionality result is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Weyl four-dimensional electromagnetism

anchor.snippet: Hermann Weyl claimed that Maxwell 's theory of electromagnetism can be expressed in terms of an action only for a four-dimensional manifold

annotations.note: Weyl's claim about four-dimensional electromagnetism is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tangherlini electron orbital instability

anchor.snippet: Tangherlini showed in 1963 that when there are more than three spatial dimensions, electron orbitals around nuclei cannot be stable

annotations.note: Tangherlini's orbital-instability result is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tegmark anthropic argument on T=1

anchor.snippet: If T differs from 1, the behavior of physical systems could not be predicted reliably from knowledge of the relevant partial differential equations

annotations.note: Tegmark's anthropic dimensionality argument is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Feng marginal dimensionality result

anchor.snippet: Wei-Xiang Feng showed that (3 + 1) -dimensional spacetime is the marginal dimensionality

annotations.note: Feng's marginal-dimensionality result is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Scargill 2D complex life

anchor.snippet: James Scargill argued that complex life may be possible with two spatial dimensions

annotations.note: Scargill's argument about 2+1 dimensions is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Minkowski space

anchor.snippet: a single four-dimensional continuum now known as Minkowski space

annotations.note: Minkowski space as a pseudo-Euclidean (1,3)-signature space is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Squared Minkowski interval formula

anchor.snippet: Then the squared spacetime interval

annotations.note: The explicit formula Δs² = (cΔt)² − Δx² for the Minkowski interval is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Metric signature conventions (+−−−) / (−+++)

anchor.snippet: These sign conventions are associated with the metric signatures

annotations.note: The two Lorentzian sign conventions for the Minkowski metric are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hyperboloid of one/two sheets

anchor.snippet: generate hyperboloids of one sheet, while the invariant hyperbolae displaced by timelike intervals from the origin generate hyperboloids of two sheets

annotations.note: Hyperboloids of one and two sheets in (1+2)-Minkowski space are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Velocity parameter β = v/c

anchor.snippet: a common shorthand for the ratio of the speed of an object relative to light

annotations.note: The dimensionless velocity parameter β = v/c is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Mass–energy equivalence E = mc²

anchor.snippet: Simplified to the case of zero velocity, this is Einstein's equation relating energy and mass

annotations.note: Einstein's mass–energy equivalence E = mc² is a physical relation not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Low-velocity expansion of γmc² yields kinetic energy

anchor.snippet: consider a series expansion of γmc 2 at low velocity

annotations.note: The series expansion recovering the classical kinetic-energy term is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lorentzian metric signature (−,+,+,+)

anchor.snippet: This means the smooth Lorentz metric

annotations.note: Lorentzian metrics with signature (−,+,+,+) are not present in Mathlib.
